author: @leonf.7893
text: I would like to make an electric skateboard. From which old appliances can I find the motor, and or rechargeable batteries?

replies.author: @mike289homebuilt5
replies.text:
Electric Skateboard is such a amazing technology.   I doubt you would ever have the long range the new boards have.   They sold a conversion kit online.    Trying to build your own things you must find parts close enough to work and that can be difficult to impossible.   
     I collect motors myself,   im trying to find a more powerful fan for my Old automobile.   Size voltage current rating.   To find battery pack revive any old ones use youtube videos online use Eye protection an common sense ohh design ur battery pak around an existing charger.   I own a E-bike for years it may be alot more easy to make e-bike or small scooter

author: @TechKnowCSS
text: OFF TOPIC.  UNLIMITED FREE ENERGY.  I WAS WONDERING IF THERE IS A WAY TO EXTRACT POWER FROM THE SIGNAL OF 4G/5G EVEN WIFI? JUST FORGET THE DATA BECAUSE THAT DON'T MATTER JUST THE SIGNAL TO ENERGY.

replies.author: @globalmedia2730
replies.text: Yes. Cut a wire to the resonant frequency you are aiming for.  (Tuned antenna), insulate it then using a Bridge rectifier (full wave) and a decent earth attach a capacitor and watch it Ever so slowly charge. Good luck using these mill amps to do anything useful though.

replies.author: @TechKnowCSS
replies.text: @globalmedia2730 ABOUT WHAT I FIGURED ON THE CIRCUIT TO USE BUT THERE MUST BE A MORE EFFICIENT WAY TO EXTRACT THE POWER WITH LESS LOSS, AND WOULD A LARGER ANTENNA LIKE 30 FEET BY 10 FEET COLLECT MORE? OR EVEN AN ARRAY OF THEM. IT MAY EVEN BE ENOUGH POWER TO PREVENT A STORED BATTERY FROM GOING DEAD(FLAT)? BUT TO CHARGE A BATTERY I DON'T KNOW. EITHER WAY IT WOULD COLLECT POWER DAY AND NIGHT NON STOP FOR FREE.

author: @jeffreywolsieffer4583
text:
hmm, I still like the Water Powered Solar-Cell myself.!
. Design by Scotti Crautor, it uses Solar to pump the water from its reservoir to the Water Generator, the Generator lights the LEDs and powers the Circulation pump to return the spent Water back into its reservoir.
. . not bad for a 12 Year Old.- JW .. .. .. btw, hows your contest going?

author: @kreynolds1123
text: I believe  most larger or chorded vacuum cleaners will use an induction motor, which can generate if spun faster than the frequency  that its field coils are excited at, be it excited with a grid connection or self excited with capacitors.

author: @ericblenner-hassett3945
text: Nice to see a Joule Thief in action.  you might want to see if after the thief there is enough to actually charge that battery pack that was in the vacuum.  you would need at least a diode, if not a full bridge for battery safety.
